title: That Weirdo Isn't Satoshi
logline: In a sleepy Suffolk village, a paranoid tracksuit-wearing crypto obsessive becomes convinced his reclusive leek-growing neighbour is Satoshi Nakamoto — until a gang of international Bitcoin hunters descends and village farce ensues.
synopsis: Baz Fletcher, divorced and permanently tracksuited, spends his days in his mum's conservatory in Little Bunting, Suffolk, refreshing blockchain explorers and shouting at YouTube. When a quiet Japanese-British leek-gardener moves in next door — socks with sandals, Marks & Spencer polo, battered Corolla — Baz is convinced he's found Satoshi Nakamoto. His viral TikTok drags in a Lamborghini-driving American whale, a Russian oligarch's daughter by helicopter, two incompetent MI6 agents, and a chain-smoking French journalist. The village fête devolves into a coconut-shy hostage situation, the parish WhatsApp group loses its mind, and Baz's ex-wife arrives demanding alimony. The big twist, in a rainy greenhouse at midnight: the 'Satoshi' is a painfully shy retired IT consultant from Croydon who just wanted to grow prize leeks in peace.
tagline: He's not the Bitcoin messiah. He's just a very naughty neighbour.
posterPrompt: Cinematic British comedy movie poster. Suffolk village high street, a paranoid middle-aged man in a grey Nike tracksuit crouches behind a hedge holding an iPhone, a serene Japanese-British gentleman in socks-with-sandals carries a tray of prize leeks in the background. A lime-green Lamborghini half-crashed into a village-fête bunting line. Painterly Hot Fuzz / Four Lions poster style, warm amber late-afternoon light, bold typography placeholder top. Portrait 2:3. Muted British palette — moss green, brick red, custard yellow — with one punch of crypto orange.
clipPrompts: Cinematic 8-second clip. Golden dawn drone shot over a sleepy Suffolk village — pebble-dashed houses, medieval church spire, allotments, one empty high street. Smooth push-forward motion. Transition to a cluttered 1970s conservatory interior: a paranoid middle-aged man in a grey Nike tracksuit and Crocs is hunched over three old monitors showing wild Bitcoin charts and blockchain explorers, empty Monster energy cans piled everywhere. He jabs a finger at the screen, wild-eyed. Warm tungsten light, handheld camera, Hot Fuzz-style British comedy tone.

Cinematic 8-second clip. A quiet Japanese-British man in his late 50s steps out of a battered 1998 Toyota Corolla in a Suffolk driveway, carrying a tray of prize leeks with both hands. He wears beige slacks, a Marks & Spencer polo, and very obvious black socks with brown sandals. Slow push-in on the socks-and-sandals as he walks calmly toward his front door. Overcast soft daylight, a suspicious tracksuited neighbour watches from behind a hedge in the background, completely still.

Cinematic 8-second clip. Chaotic arrival montage in a quiet English village high street. A lime-green Lamborghini screeches in, tyres smoking. Smash cut: a black helicopter lands on a cricket pitch, rotor wash flattening the bunting. Smash cut: a glamorous woman in a full-length fur coat steps out, chin high. Smash cut: two awkward British agents in bad suits pretending to read a bus timetable. Smash cut: a chain-smoking French journalist with a vintage Leica. Villagers stare, the vicar rings his bicycle bell once in horror. Fast comedic cuts, bright daylight, British farce energy.

Cinematic 8-second clip. Heavy rain, midnight. Torch beams sweep across a small glass greenhouse lit from within by warm amber light. Inside: a crowd of soaked ridiculous characters are crammed between tomato plants. At the centre, a calm Japanese-British gentleman stands among his prize leeks holding a single piece of paper, mid-sentence, mild expression. Slow dramatic push-in on his face, then a quick cut to a tracksuited man in the crowd whose world has just quietly collapsed — devastated deadpan. Dead silence implied. Moody blue-night exterior, warm orange interior — British kitchen-sink cinema.
voScript: In a sleepy Suffolk village… a paranoid man. A viral hunch. And one very suspicious pair of sandals. When a single TikTok sets the world of cryptocurrency on fire, only one question matters. Is he — or isn't he — Satoshi Nakamoto? He's not the Bitcoin messiah. He's just a very naughty neighbour.
musicPrompt: Up-tempo British ska-punk instrumental — skanking electric guitar, bright trumpet stabs, pub-rock drums, a hint of folk fiddle. Cinematic trailer bed at 138 BPM. Mischievous and slightly manic energy, Hot Fuzz meets The Full Monty. Brief moody minor-key breakdown around twenty seconds in, then resolves triumphantly. Stereo, no vocals, no spoken word.
